steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The student's goal is to emphasize a similarity between the two sculptures: Seated Woman (1962) and Woman Fixing Her Hair (1993). To accomplish this goal, a choice must reference both sculptures and identify something they share.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘*Seated Woman* is a mahogany sculpture that celebrated artist Elizabeth Catlett created in 1962.’: This discusses only Seated Woman. It mentions just one of the two sculptures and therefore cannot establish a similarity between them. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Elizabeth Catlett was a celebrated artist who created prints as well as sculptures.’: This describes Catlett's general body of work, not either specific sculpture. It doesn't address the two sculptures at all, let alone compare them. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Elizabeth Catlett's sculptures *Seated Woman* and *Woman Fixing Her Hair* are both made of mahogany.’: This names both sculptures and explicitly states what they share — mahogany as their material. This directly accomplishes the goal of emphasizing a similarity between the two. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Elizabeth Catlett, a celebrated artist, was born in 1915.’: This is a biographical detail about the artist and mentions neither sculpture. It does not establish any similarity between the two works. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
